NWS SURVEY TEAM CONCLUDED THAT AN EF0 TORNADO BRIEFLY TOUCHED DOWN 3 TIMES ON A PATH ALONG HIGHWAY 1 BETWEEN PATRICK AND CHERAW. MAXIMUM WINDS WERE ESTIMATED TO RANGE FROM 65 TO 70 MPH. THERE WERE SEVERAL TREES DOWN. TOP SNAPPED OUT OF A TREE. NO STRUCTURAL DAMAGE EVIDENT.

JACKS CREEK LANDING AND CAMPGROUND REPORTED VERY STRONG WINDS FOR APPROXIMATELY 5 MINUTES. THE STRONG WINDS BLEW SEVERAL MEDIUM TO LARGE LIMBS DOWN ALONG WITH SEVERAL TRASH CANS WITHIN THE CAMPGROUND. THE WINDS ALSO PRODUCED LARGE WAVES THAT CAUSED A BOATS BOW LINE TO SNAP WHILE ANCHORED TO THE DOCK. .

COUNTY DOT OFFICIAL SAID THAT HEAVY RAIN CAUSED THOMPSON CREEK TO FLOOD. THE CREEK ROSE OVER THE BRIDGE ON HORNSBORO ROAD NEAR RUDOLPH SIKES ROAD. THE CREEK IS USUALLY 6 FEET BELOW THE BRIDGE. THE HEAVY RAIN CAUSED THE WIDTH OF THE CREEK TO INCREASE APPROXIMATELY 150 TO 200 FEET WIDE AT THE BRIDGE. RADAR ESTIMATES INDICATE THAT 4 TO 6 INCHES OF RAIN FELL IN THE CREEK BASIN. THE DURATION OF THE FLASH FLOOD EVENT WAS 30 MINUTES.
